ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for a 22 oz fountain soft drink; FOODON_03301099 (carbonated beverage) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW covers carbonated soft drinks as a category; no size-specific fountain drink code exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 represents carbonated soft drink as a category; used as closest parent for this fountain drink serving.

serving.natural_language_portion: One 22-fluid-ounce (approximately 651 ml) fountain cup of carbonated soft drink, typically dispensed at a fast-food or sandwich restaurant counter.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, carbonated, cola, contains caffeine — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 14400)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values are based on USDA SR Legacy entry for regular cola (FDC ID 14400), which is the most representative generic carbonated soft drink available in an approved source. A 22 oz fountain drink is a serving-size variant of this product category. Per-100g values are taken directly from the USDA entry. Nutrient profile is representative of a standard cola-type fountain drink; actual values vary by syrup flavour dispensed.

gi_source.title: Coca-Cola, GI value from University of Sydney GI Database — Sugary carbonated beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 63 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for regular cola-type carbonated soft drinks (Coca-Cola reference, GI ~63, medium category). A 22 oz (651 g) serving contains approximately 62.5 g of available carbohydrate (9.6 g per 100g × 6.51). Glycemic load per serving = (63 × 62.5) / 100 = 39.4; however, using the full 651 ml serving weight: GL = (63 × 9.6 × 6.51) / 100 ≈ 39.4. Rounded to 39.4 and documented. Note: GL is high due to large serving size. This is an analogous estimate applied to a generic fountain soft drink; actual GI may vary by syrup type.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar load and moderate sodium; regular consumption linked to elevated blood pressure via insulin resistance and weight gain pathways
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid regular carbonated soft drinks due to their high free-sugar content, which contributes to weight gain and insulin resistance, both of which elevate blood pressure; a 22 oz serving delivers approximately 62 g of added sugar, far exceeding the WHO daily limit of 25 g.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high free-sugar content and high glycemic load per serving drive rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid regular fountain soft drinks; a single 22 oz serving contains approximately 62 g of rapidly absorbed sugars with a glycemic load of approximately 39, causing a sharp postprandial glucose spike that is incompatible with glycaemic management goals.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free-sugar intake from regular consumption is associated with elevated triglycerides, visceral adiposity, and increased cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should minimise regular carbonated soft drink consumption; the high free-sugar load in a 22 oz serving contributes to hypertriglyceridaemia and metabolic syndrome, both established cardiovascular risk factors, and should be replaced with water or unsweetened beverages.

llm_training.natural_language_description: A 22 oz (651 ml) fountain soft drink is a large-format carbonated beverage dispensed from a soda fountain machine, commonly served at fast-food restaurants, sandwich chains such as Subway, and convenience outlets in Singapore and globally. The drink is typically a cola, lemon-lime soda, orange soda, or similar flavoured carbonated beverage made from carbonated water, high-fructose corn syrup or sucrose, caramel colour, phosphoric or citric acid, and natural or artificial flavourings. It is served chilled, often over ice, and is characterised by its effervescent, sweet taste with a slight acidic bite. Nutritionally, a 22 oz serving delivers approximately 241 kcal and 62 g of free sugars, with negligible protein, fat, fibre, and micronutrients. The glycemic index of a standard cola is approximately 63 (medium), but the glycemic load per 22 oz serving is very high at approximately 39, making it clinically significant for blood glucose management. Regular consumption is associated with increased risk of Type 2 Diabetes, obesity, hypertriglyceridaemia, and dental caries. It is not a nutritionally recommended beverage for any health condition. In Singapore, fountain drinks are commonly offered as part of meal combos at Western fast-food and sandwich outlets, priced around SGD 3.30. The food is vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free, but is ultra-processed and provides only empty calories.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a 22oz fountain drink?
Is a large soda bad for diabetes?
How much sugar is in a 22oz Subway drink?
Nutritional value of large carbonated soft drink
Can I drink soda if I have hypertension?
How many grams of sugar in a large fountain drink?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy for generic regular cola (FDC ID 14400), which is the closest approved-source match for a fountain carbonated soft drink. The actual nutritional profile of a 22 oz fountain drink at Subway Singapore may vary depending on the syrup flavour selected (cola, lemon-lime, orange, etc.). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for cola. No Singapore-specific HPB Foodo entry exists for this product. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. protein_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 14400); carbonated soft drinks contain no protein by formulation — confirmed zero. 2. fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; carbonated soft drinks contain no fat by formulation — confirmed zero. 3. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero — naturally zero as this is a fat-free sugar-water beverage. 4. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero — naturally zero as carbonated soft drinks contain no dietary fibre. 5. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg are all confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy — confirmed zero as regular cola contains negligible vitamins. 6. glycemic_index set to 63 (medium) based on University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate for cola-type carbonated soft drink. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (63 × 9.6 × 6.51) / 100 ≈ 39.4 for a 651 g serving. 7. Input food_group was 'Vegetables' — corrected to 'Beverages' as this is a carbonated soft drink. Input cuisine was 'Sandwich' — corrected to 'Western' as the drink itself is a Western-origin product. 8. The 22 oz serving size corresponds to approximately 651 ml (1 fl oz ≈ 29.57 ml; 22 × 29.57 = 650.5 ml). 9. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the food is served in Singapore. 10. No HPB Singapore Foodo entry found for fountain soft drinks; USDA SR Legacy used as primary approved source.
